stories_covered: Chamath announces new SPAC with crypto-age disclaimer
CoreWeave IPO at $11.2B with $22B annual CapEx
NewBank earnings surge: $2.5B net income, 42% YoY growth
Revolut fintech strategy with 26 unreleased products
On reaching $4B ARR at 38% YoY growth
harry_takes: The era makes $100B private valuations commonplace—this marks a shift in how we value infrastructure at scale
Peak bubble isn't knowable in real time; SPACs returning correlates strongly with prior bubble in 2021, suggesting frothy sentiment
Databricks at 25X run rate feels reasonably priced IF growth persists 2-3 more years at 40%+ rates
CoreWeave depends entirely on sustained AI CapEx; if demand slows, they become a canary in the coal mine before public hyperscalers feel it
harry_boldest_quote: One of the things I'm learning especially on this podcast is to not try and fill the need to have an opinion just to fill the space.
predictions: Lemkin: Within 12-24 months, there's a one-in-three chance AI CapEx demand slows, compressing valuations like CoreWeave
Stebbings: If Databricks IPOs next year at $200B, and growth continues, it will validate the current $100B private valuation
Panel: If the AI boom continues 2-3 more years, Databricks will compound into 8-9X run rate valuations and feel reasonable on traditional metrics
disagreements: Jason argues CoreWeave's debt matters less if take-or-pay contracts are ironclad with Microsoft/OpenAI; Rory counters that growth rate assumptions are fragile and paying-rather-than-taking signals early demand weakness.
harry_role: Harry moderates and challenges both panelists with follow-up questions, deferring on valuation judgments ('not to fill the space') while pressing on CoreWeave's debt structure and fintech durability. He frames macro trends but lets Lemkin and O'Driscoll drive conclusions.
notable: Harry opens by declaring his rule to avoid filling airtime with empty opinions—a self-aware meta-moment about podcast discipline—right before a lengthy debate on whether CoreWeave's debt and AI demand are sustainable.
